candidates.title: The Local Inhibition Graph: A Neuroscience-Inspired Training-Free Diagnostic for Feature Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
candidates.summary: First connection between LCA lateral inhibition and SAE absorption. Shows W_dec^T W_dec = G_LCA (exact structural correspondence). Constructs local inhibition graph from decoder correlations and tests whether graph edges predict known absorption pairs. Explains precision-recall asymmetry (H5) via competitive suppression. Proposes training-free homeostatic rebalancing repair. Scalable to million-latent SAEs.
hypotheses: H6: Graph edges predict absorption pairs with precision@20 >= 0.10 (vs ~0.004 chance)
H7: Inhibition explains precision-recall asymmetry (recall correlates with inhibition, precision does not)
H8: Total incoming inhibition predicts at-risk features (r > 0.3)
H9: Graph structure is layer-dependent (mean edge weight increases with depth)
H10: Homeostatic rebalancing restores parent firing (recon error < 5%)
candidates.pilot_focus: Construct inhibition graph for GPT-2 Small SAE (24K latents, k=20 neighbors). Validate precision@k against Chanin absorption pairs on 26 first-letter features. Test precision-recall asymmetry explanation. Target: ~15 min graph construction + ~15 min validation.
candidates.novelty_claim: First LCA-SAE connection (exact: W_dec^T W_dec = G_LCA). First local inhibition graph for SAE diagnostics. First mechanistic explanation for precision-recall asymmetry. First training-free post-hoc repair for absorption.
risk_factors: Graph edges may not correspond to absorption pairs (structural correspondence could fail empirically)
Homeostatic rebalancing may degrade reconstruction or create artifacts
Neuroscience analogy may be criticized as superficial (mitigation: exact mathematical correspondence)
Gemma-2-2B access issues may block cross-model validation
mitigation: Use local graph (top-k neighbors) for scalability; O(k * d_dict) not O(d_dict^2)
Constrain repair to preserve reconstruction: ||a - W_dec*z'||_2 <= (1+epsilon) * ||a - W_dec*z||_2
Ground claims in LCA mathematical framework (Rozell et al. 2008, Eq. 3)
If repair fails, diagnostic contribution (graph edges flag absorption) stands independently
Primary experiments on GPT-2 Small; Gemma as validation only
prior_evidence_used: H5 (precision invariant, recall variable) --- explained by competitive suppression
H1b (delta-corrected correlation at layer 8) --- explained by layer-dependent inhibition strength
Feature U (24.2% abs, 100% steering) --- explained by decoder alignment preservation
EC50 null result --- explained by inhibition affecting activation, not decoder geometry

candidates.title: Does Feature Absorption Matter? A Metric-Specific, Layer-Dependent Null Result on Downstream SAE Reliability
candidates.summary: PREVIOUS FRONT-RUNNER (iter_001-008). First systematic study connecting feature absorption to downstream task performance. Raw metrics show no correlation, but delta-corrected steering reveals significant negative correlation at layer 8 (r=-0.431, p=0.028 uncorrected). Precision-recall decomposition shows absorption affects recall but not precision. EC50 analysis shows no efficiency degradation. Result debate score: 3/10. Verdict: PIVOT.
hypotheses: H1: Raw steering does not significantly correlate with absorption (FALSIFIED)
H1b: Delta-corrected steering significantly correlates with absorption at layer 8 (PARTIALLY SUPPORTED: uncorrected p=0.028, Bonferroni p=0.334)
H2: Probing F1 does not correlate with absorption (FALSIFIED)
H3: Cross-layer consistency fails (FALSIFIED: opposite-sign slopes)
H4: Absorption affects steering efficiency, not capability (NOT SUPPORTED - EC50 analysis shows no significant efficiency degradation)
H5: Precision is unaffected by absorption; only recall varies (SUPPORTED)
candidates.pilot_focus: Completed. 26 first-letter features (A-Z), GPT-2 Small res-jb SAE, layers 0/4/8/10. Steering + probing + random baseline + EC50 + precision-recall + cross-model Pythia-70M.
candidates.novelty_claim: First systematic correlation between absorption and downstream tasks. Critical methodological insight: baseline correction is essential. Precision-recall decomposition reveals mechanism.
risk_factors: Zero significant results after multiple comparison correction
Single model (GPT-2 Small) limits generalizability; Pythia-70M cross-validation inconclusive
Effect size is modest (R^2=0.19 for H1b)
Concurrent work challenges SAE premise broadly (Sanity Checks, DeepMind negative results)
Result debate score 3/10, recommended PIVOT
mitigation: Reframe as context/background for new front-runner rather than primary contribution
Use existing data to validate inhibition graph predictions (H7, H8)
Retain precision-recall decomposition as supporting evidence

evidence_quality.statistical_significance: None after correction --- H1b uncorrected p=0.028, Bonferroni p=0.334, BH-FDR q=0.107
evidence_quality.methodological_rigor: Improved --- random baseline added, EC50 computed, precision-recall decomposed, MCP applied
evidence_quality.generalizability: Poor --- single model primary, cross-model inconclusive

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Optimal Compression: A Trade-off Analysis
candidates.summary: Reframe absorption as a predictable consequence of the interpretability-compression trade-off. Quantify how absorption-fixing interventions increase other pathologies (dead neurons, hedging, reconstruction error). Supported by evidence that absorption coexists with functional steering and perfect precision. Result debate recommended this as pivot target.
hypotheses: Absorption-reducing interventions increase other pathologies quantifiably
Pareto frontier exists for (reconstruction, sparsity, absorption)
No SAE architecture dominates on all three metrics simultaneously
candidates.pilot_focus: Compare standard SAE vs OrtSAE on GPT-2 (layer 8). Measure absorption + dead neuron rate + reconstruction error. Target: ~1 GPU-hour.
candidates.novelty_claim: First quantification of absorption-pathology trade-off. First three-way Pareto frontier for SAE evaluation.
risk_factors: OrtSAE checkpoints may be unavailable for GPT-2
Trade-off may not be quantifiable with existing metrics
Results may be too negative to be publishable
mitigation: Use SAEBench collection with different architectures as proxy
Frame as 'rethinking' rather than 'debunking'
If mainstream view is correct, that's also publishable

candidates.title: The Local Inhibition Graph: A Neuroscience-Inspired Training-Free Diagnostic and Repair for Feature Absorption
candidates.summary: ORIGINAL INTERDISCIPLINARY PROPOSAL. Model SAE activation dynamics as competitive neural network with inhibition graph from decoder correlations (W_dec^T W_dec = G_LCA). Apply homeostatic repair to rebalance activations and restore general feature firing. Now merged into front-runner cand_f with refined scope.
hypotheses: H1 (Diagnostic): Graph edges correspond to known absorption pairs with precision significantly above chance
H2 (Repair): Homeostatic rebalancing restores parent firing without degrading reconstruction
H3 (Utility): Repaired features show improved encoder-dependent task performance
candidates.pilot_focus: Construct inhibition graph for GPT-2 Small SAE (24K latents, k=20 neighbors). Validate precision@k against Chanin absorption pairs. Target: ~15 min.
candidates.novelty_claim: First connection between LCA lateral inhibition and SAE absorption. First training-free post-hoc repair. First local inhibition graph for SAE diagnostics.
risk_factors: Inhibition graph computation may be numerically unstable
Repair may degrade reconstruction or create artifacts
Neuroscience analogy may be criticized as superficial
mitigation: Use local graph (top-k neighbors) for scalability
Constrain repair to preserve reconstruction
Ground claims in LCA mathematical framework
If repair fails, diagnostic contribution stands independently

candidates.title: Scaling Laws for Feature Absorption Rate in Sparse Autoencoders
candidates.summary: Propose and empirically validate a scaling law for feature absorption rate as a function of dictionary size, sparsity level, and layer depth. Predicts non-monotonic inverted-U curve. Dropped due to phenomenological nature and lack of theoretical grounding compared to inhibition graph.
hypotheses: Absorption rate follows power law with exponential saturation: A(N) = alpha_0 * N^{beta_N} * exp(-gamma * N / N^*(L))
Critical dictionary size N^* exists beyond which absorption decreases
Layer depth increases absorption up to middle layers then decreases
candidates.pilot_focus: Measure absorption on Gemma Scope SAEs at 16K and 65K (layer 12). Fit preliminary scaling curve. Target: ~1 GPU-hour.
candidates.novelty_claim: First scaling law for absorption rate. First characterization of non-monotonic relationship between dictionary size and absorption.
risk_factors: Power-law form is phenomenological, not derived from first principles
Measurement noise from SAEBench metric may bias estimates
Model-specific effects may prevent cross-family generalization
mitigation: Use multiple dictionary sizes from same training pipeline (Gemma Scope)
Report confidence intervals and model comparison
Validate on Pythia-160M as cross-model check

candidates.title: Beyond First Letters: A Validated, Generalized Metric for Feature Absorption
candidates.summary: Develop and validate an enhanced absorption metric that detects partial absorption, multi-latent compensation, and semantic hierarchy features. Validates against SynthSAEBench ground truth. Dropped because the inhibition graph provides a more principled and novel approach than metric engineering.
hypotheses: Enhanced metric detects 2-3x more absorption instances than Chanin metric
Enhanced metric maintains zero false positives on non-hierarchical features
Both metrics agree on ground-truth synthetic data
candidates.pilot_focus: Run original + enhanced metric on Gemma Scope 16K (layer 12). Compare detected cases. Target: ~15 min.
candidates.novelty_claim: First validation of absorption metric against ground truth. First generalization beyond first-letter tasks.
risk_factors: Enhanced metric may have high false positive rate
Semantic hierarchy probes may be unreliable
Effect size may be smaller than expected
mitigation: Rigorous negative control testing
Multiple probe types for semantic features
Pre-register analysis plan; report null results honestly

pivot_conditions.front_runner_to_c: H6 fails (graph edges do not predict absorption pairs). Trade-off analysis becomes the natural fallback.
pivot_conditions.front_runner_to_alternatives: H6 partially validated (precision@20 = 0.05-0.10). Proceed with diagnostic-only claims, drop repair.
pivot_conditions.any_to_null_result: All hypotheses fail. Write rigorous null-result cautionary tale paper for workshop/arXiv.
pivot_conditions.front_runner_strengthened: H6 validated AND H10 (repair) succeeds. Paper becomes significantly stronger with both diagnostic and corrective contributions.

novelty_searches: Web: 'local inhibition graph' sparse autoencoder feature absorption 2025 2026 --- NO MATCHES
Web: 'decoder correlation' 'feature absorption' SAE repair homeostastic 2025 2026 --- NO MATCHES for homeostatic repair
Web: Rozell LCA 'locally competitive algorithm' sparse autoencoder decoder correlation W_dec 2024 2025 --- NO MATCHES for LCA-SAE connection
Web: 'partial information decomposition' PID sparse autoencoder feature absorption 2025 2026 --- NO MATCHES for PID-SAE connection

Web: 'absorption' 'Pareto frontier' 'trade-off' sparse autoencoder SAE reconstruction sparsity 2025 2026 --- Partial matches (Matryoshka SAEs, AdaptiveK) but no direct three-way trade-off formalization

novelty_verification.lca_sae_connection: NO PRIOR WORK found connecting Rozell et al.'s LCA to SAE absorption. Rozell (2008) has ~2000 citations but zero applications to LLM SAEs. The structural correspondence W_dec^T W_dec = G_LCA has not been articulated.
novelty_verification.local_inhibition_graph: NO PRIOR WORK found constructing a graph from decoder correlations for SAE absorption diagnosis. No paper in the SAE literature mentions 'inhibition graph' or 'decoder correlation graph'.
novelty_verification.homeostatic_repair: NO PRIOR WORK found proposing training-free post-hoc repair for absorption. All existing solutions (Matryoshka, OrtSAE, ATM, Balance Matryoshka) require retraining.
novelty_verification.pid_sae: NO PRIOR WORK found applying Partial Information Decomposition to SAE absorption. PID has been used in neuroscience for neural population coding but not for SAE interpretability.

synthesis_metadata.verdict: PIVOT from correlation study (cand_a, score 3/10) to Local Inhibition Graph framework (cand_f). The inhibition graph provides a genuinely novel theoretical contribution, explains existing findings mechanistically, and offers a practical diagnostic tool --- all while being training-free and scalable.
evidence_driven_revisions: Previous front-runner (cand_a) dropped due to 3/10 result debate score and zero significant results after MCP
Interdisciplinary Candidate C (LCA inhibition graph) promoted to front-runner (cand_f)
Innovator Candidate B (PID) dropped due to computational complexity; inhibition graph achieves similar explanatory power more cleanly
Theoretical Candidate C (Pareto frontier) retained as backup (cand_c)
All prior hypotheses (H1-H5) retained as context/background for new framework
New hypotheses (H6-H10) derived from inhibition graph framework with clear falsification criteria

perspective_weighting.interdisciplinary: Highest weight --- exact structural correspondence (W_dec^T W_dec = G_LCA), specific falsifiable predictions, training-free and scalable
perspective_weighting.innovator: Highest weight --- independently identified same gap, cross-domain insight (neuroscience -> SAEs)
perspective_weighting.contrarian: Strong influence --- 'absorption is benign' finding preserved and explained mechanistically via competitive suppression
perspective_weighting.theoretical: Strong influence --- rate-distortion context (absorption as optimal compression) provides broader framework
perspective_weighting.empiricist: Moderate influence --- rigorous controls and falsifiable predictions ensure testability
perspective_weighting.pragmatist: Moderate influence --- training-free, scalable, practical diagnostic tool
